etymology.description: 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") + four 口(口) (kǒu, "vessel") -> depiction of a dog guarding vessels
etymology.interpretation: Traditionally, 犬(犬) (quǎn, "dog") represents protection, while 口(口) (kǒu, "vessel") represents containers; together, they depict a dog guarding multiple vessels, symbolizing tools or implements collectively arranged. Although the etymology is debated, this visualization captures the essence of items organized for use.

examples.chinese: 她用了很多工具(她用了很多工具)来修理这台机器(來修理這台機器)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòngle hěn duō gōngjù lái xiūlǐ zhè tái jīqì.
examples.english: She used many tools to repair this machine.

examples.chinese: 厨房里的器具(廚房裡的器具)非常齐全(非常齊全)。
examples.pinyin: Chúfáng lǐ de qìjù fēicháng qíquán.
examples.english: The kitchen utensils are very complete.

examples.chinese: 他用一把笛子(他用一把笛子)演奏了一首优美的乐曲(演奏了一首優美的樂曲)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng yī bǎ dízi yǎnzòule yī shǒu yōuměi de yuèqǔ.
examples.english: He played a beautiful piece of music with a flute.
